claim: FAO reports Sub-Saharan African cereal yields average 1.5 t/ha versus 5.6 in East Asia, with soil degradation, limited fertilizer use (average 15 kg/ha versus 150 globally), and rainfed dependence as primary constraints.
method: Reviewed FAOSTAT crop production and yield data for Sub-Saharan Africa.
limitations: FAOSTAT data is reported at national level, smoothing sub-national variation.
Smallholder yields may differ from national averages.

claim: The Global Yield Gap Atlas estimates water-limited yield potential for maize at 8-12 t/ha in Sub-Saharan Africa versus actual yields of 1-3 t/ha, indicating a yield gap of 70-85 percent attributable to nutrient and water limitations.
method: Reviewed GYGA methodology, crop model descriptions, and Sub-Saharan Africa yield gap estimates.
limitations: Water-limited potential assumes optimal nutrient supply, overestimating attainable yield on degraded soils.
Sub-national estimates depend on climate data quality.
